name: Libya

colors.symbolism: the blood shed by Libyans in the resistance against foreign occupation
the dark days of Italian colonisation; the historical flag of the Senussi Emirate of Cyrenaica
Libya's wealth of agriculture and prosperity, and Islam
the crescent and star, traditional symbols of Islam

description: Three horizontal stripes of red, black and green in a 1:2:1 ratio, with a white crescent and five-pointed star centred on the wider black band.
history: Originally adopted on 24 December 1951 as the flag of the Kingdom of Libya, designed by Omar Faiek Shennib and approved by King Idris al-Senussi. It was replaced after the 1969 coup by Muammar Gaddafi (first by an Arab Liberation flag and from 1977 by a plain green field). It was readopted on 3 August 2011 by the National Transitional Council during the Libyan civil war and confirmed as the national flag after Gaddafi's fall.

features.symbolism: the colours of the Senussi dynasty and the original 1951 flag of the Kingdom of Libya
Islam, the principal religion of Libya
hope, faith and the principles of liberty, justice and brotherhood
